chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male presented with severe abdominal pain and vomiting.
history: The patient had a history of hypertriglyceridemia and was admitted with acute pancreatitis. His triglyceride concentration was initially reported as 1.53 mmol/L, but subsequent retesting revealed an actual value of 88.92 mmol/L.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 24 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed tenderness in the upper abdomen.
labs: Triglyceride concentration: 88.92 mmol/L, Lipemic index: high.
correct_diagnosis: Falsely decreased triglyceride concentration due to insufficient configuration of alarm rules.
differentials: Falsely decreased triglyceride concentration due to insufficient configuration of alarm rules.
correct_ranking: Falsely decreased triglyceride concentration due to insufficient configuration of alarm rules.
